message:
refactor(acp): modernize Rust idioms in ACP session/prompt handlers

Replace verbose if-let-else blocks with idiomatic combinators and
let-else syntax throughout the ACP (Agent Client Protocol) module:

- Lock-guard session lookups: `if let Ok(guard)...else { None/false }`
  → `map_or_else(|_| None, ...)` / `map_or(false, ...)`
- Parameter extraction: `match ... Some(c) => c, None => { error; continue }`
  → `let Some(contents) = ... else { error; continue }`
- Agent session acquisition: `match ... Some(session) => session, None => { notify; return }`
  → `let Some(agent) = ... else { notify; return }`
- Write macro: `write!(prompt, "- ...\n")` → `writeln!(prompt, "- ...")`
- Borrow elision: `serde_json::to_value(&msg.stop_reason)` drops unnecessary `&`

No behavioral changes — purely stylistic, reducing nesting depth and
line count while preserving identical control flow and error handling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
